reviews.review_title: Great use of MR and great storytelling
reviews.review_description: Awesome idea to put your own environnement to use. This enhances the empathy we feel, especially as the experience talk about what we can call home. Very powerful.

This experience will not leave your eyes dry. Welcoming you into an Ukrainian home, you will share a moment- devastating but beautiful, with what used to be a safe warm place that was torn to pieces by war and violence. I first saw it at Anidox XR at the Viborg animation festival in Denmark and cried. Just tried it at home and cried again. Sensitive and beautiful. You need to try it.

It’s a deeply moving experience. Using very simple tools, yet incredibly powerful, precisely because of that simplicity. By using ordinary objects from our everyday lives — a table, bread, a book, a cat — and layering them onto the actual room we are physically in as participants, everything feels incredibly personal. Stays with you long after it’s over.
I'd suggest watching it at your own home, alone.

reviews.review_title: An emotional tale perfect MR use.
reviews.review_description:
It is a very polished VR story, with an amazing idea to make it your own through mixed reality.

I loved the idea to make visual elements and song work togheter. As layers of the room appear so do layers of voices.

While we all know the story by now, or some similar one especially in Eastern Europe, as it's not singular but one recurring arround us and in news for years now, unfortunately, this experience makes that common knowledge a tool of understanding, belonging and empathy.

Amazing experience about what it means when your home is in danger because of war. We showcased the project at Art*VR festival, where it got great recognition. Don't hesitate to try it in your own environment!
The experience is such a wonderful example of the emotional impact XR can have when innovative use of technology meets sensitive storytelling. 30 minutes well spent!
Poetic and strong and example of MR storytelling.

A truly powerful and emotional experience. The way the story unfolds through mixed reality makes it feel deeply personal and immersive. The voices, traditional melodies, and atmosphere create a strong sense of connection and reflection about what “home” really means.

Huge congratulations to Ondřej Moravec for creating something so thoughtful and moving. Experiences like this show how meaningful and impactful VR can be. Truly unforgettable.

The first time I experienced Fragile Home was when it premiered at the Venice Biennale, in 2024. It was/is remarkable to witness the evolution of storytelling as technology evolves at seemingly whiplash speeds. The piece is a beautiful example that demonstrates how storytelling has indeed, become story-living.

Almost a year and a half later, I experienced it again in my own living room which wielded a much deeper impact than at a foreign location. After a quick mapping, my living room transformed. I walked around and explored my new Ukrainian surroundings, petted the cat that began purring loudly, and interacted with personal objects as the sun streamed through the door that opens to bucolic farmland...Then, the Russians invaded. My living room morphed into what felt like a pile of rubble. Sadness and much anger. Did the cat survive? How about the family?

The 20+ minute experience ultimately ends with a glimmer of hope; one that we can all use now. Ondřej Moravec and Viktoria Lopukhina are at the tip-of-the the storytelling spear; doing it with empathy and courage. Fragile Home and its creators are very deserving of the prestigious recognition that they have received, thus far. Slava Ukraini.

The mixed reality mapping worked very well with my living room. It was uncanny to see my own home blended with the narrative. Very very cool and moving. Also the music was a standout element. And I liked that you could pet the cat and hear it purr.

Very powerful and important project that transports you to Ukraine, into a reality, that because of war, many lived or still live in. You go from warm and safe home to destroyed one in a few moments. You can really feel how fragile your own life and safety is. 
The project is even more powerful now that you’re able to see it in your own home, your own safe space. I think it made cry even more than at a festival when I’ve seen it for the first time. Thank you for it, VR experiences like this one can really deliver something that matters.

This short experience is a gentle reminder of how the things we see and perceive through our tv and phones also exist in the small details of somebody's life. 

It lets you silently reflect in the comfort of your own home about the real aspects of conflict. 
Maybe asking you the question, how would your home look like? 

Got to pet a cat 5 Stars!

It is crazy to see your house transform into a Ukrainian home and the trace a family's home and experience during the wall. Very well done experience to bring the reality of a house torn about home. ANd beautiful singing!  leo

reviews.review_title: An experience on home and belonging
reviews.review_description: A stunning immersive piece with a story painfully relevant today. Don't miss it.
